# [InstCombine] `ninf` should not be propagated when folding `fcmp pred, x - y, 0` to `fcmp pred, x, y`

**Author:** dtcxzyw
**URL:** https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/issues/161525
**Status:** Closed
**Labels:** miscompilation, llvm:instcombine, floating-point, generated by fuzzer
**Closed Date:** 2025-10-02T17:44:05Z

## Body

Reproducer: https://alive2.llvm.org/ce/z/ZHW6-B
```
; bin/opt -passes=instcombine test.ll -S
define i1 @src1(float %x, float %y) {
  %fs = fsub float %x, %y
  %cmp = fcmp ninf ule float %fs, 0.000000e+00
  ret i1 %cmp
}
```
```
=>
define i1 @src1(float %x, float %y) {
#0:
  %cmp = fcmp ninf ule float %x, %y
  ret i1 %cmp
}
Transformation doesn't verify!

ERROR: Target is more poisonous than source

Example:
float %x = #x7f800000 (+oo)
float %y = #x7f800080 (SNaN)

Source:
float %fs = #xffc00003 (QNaN)
i1 %cmp = #x1 (1)

Target:
i1 %cmp = poison
Source value: #x1 (1)
Target value: poison
```
